definition: Gold trading $400-600/oz above real-rate model fair value, mechanistically linked to central bank buying (~1,000+ tonnes/year), proposed as a monetary regime uncertainty diagnostic. Called 'the most creative claim' but limited by three-episode evidence base and 1-2 quarter reporting lag on central bank purchases.
evidence: Residual is observable and mechanistically linked to central bank buying
1-2 quarter reporting lag on central bank purchases limits real-time utility
Causal direction unresolved: gold leading vol vs both responding to common factors
open_questions: What is the precise dating of the lead relationship between gold residual and vol transitions?
Is gold leading vol or are both responding to common monetary regime factors with different lags?
Can the 1-2 quarter reporting lag be overcome with proxies?
